annotations.label: Discrete Fourier transform (DFT)

anchor.snippet: The DFT is defined by the formula

annotations.note: `ZMod.dft` defines the discrete Fourier transform on `ZMod N` via the standard additive character, matching the DFT formula.

annotations.label: Fast Fourier transform (FFT)

anchor.snippet: An FFT is any method to compute the same results

annotations.note: Mathlib has no FFT algorithm or computational complexity formalization; only the mathematical DFT is defined.

annotations.label: Direct DFT evaluation cost

anchor.snippet: Evaluating this definition directly requires

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize arithmetic operation counts or computational complexity of the DFT.

annotations.label: Cooley–Tukey algorithm

anchor.snippet: This is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that recursively breaks down a DFT of any composite size

annotations.note: No grep hits for `Cooley` or `Tukey` in any FFT context in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Prime-factor (Good–Thomas) algorithm

anchor.snippet: one can use the prime-factor (Good–Thomas) algorithm (PFA), based on the Chinese remainder theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has CRT but no formalization of the Good–Thomas PFA decomposition of the DFT.

annotations.label: Rader–Brenner algorithm

anchor.snippet: The Rader–Brenner algorithm (1976)

annotations.note: No occurrences of Rader–Brenner in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Bruun's algorithm

anchor.snippet: Bruun's algorithm , in particular, is based on interpreting the FFT as a recursive factorization

annotations.note: No occurrences of Bruun's algorithm in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Winograd FFT algorithm

anchor.snippet: Another polynomial viewpoint is exploited by the Winograd FFT algorithm

annotations.note: No occurrences of Winograd in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Winograd minimal-multiplication bound

anchor.snippet: Winograd showed that the DFT can be computed with only

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize multiplication-count lower bounds for the DFT.

annotations.label: Rader's algorithm

anchor.snippet: Rader's algorithm , exploiting the existence of a generator for the multiplicative group modulo prime

annotations.note: Mathlib has primitive roots modulo p but no formalization of Rader's prime-size FFT reduction.

annotations.label: Bluestein's chirp-z algorithm

anchor.snippet: Another prime-size FFT is due to L. I. Bluestein, and is sometimes called the chirp-z algorithm

annotations.note: No occurrences of Bluestein or chirp-z in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Hexagonal FFT (HFFT)

anchor.snippet: Hexagonal fast Fourier transform (HFFT) aims at computing an efficient FFT for the hexagonally-sampled data

annotations.note: No hexagonal FFT formalization in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Real-input DFT symmetry

anchor.section: FFT algorithms specialized for real or symmetric data
anchor.snippet: the input data for the DFT are purely real, in which case the outputs satisfy the symmetry

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Analysis/Fourier/ZMod.lean` has `dft_even_iff`/`dft_odd_iff` but no conjugate-symmetry lemma for real-valued inputs.

annotations.label: Winograd lower bound on real multiplications

anchor.section: Bounds on complexity and operation counts
anchor.snippet: tight

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain complexity lower bounds for arithmetic circuits computing the DFT.

annotations.label: Morgenstern lower bound on additions

anchor.section: Bounds on complexity and operation counts
anchor.snippet: Morgenstern

annotations.note: No formalization of Morgenstern's Ω(N log N) addition lower bound in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Pan asynchronicity lower bound

anchor.section: Bounds on complexity and operation counts

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Pan's asynchronicity-based FFT lower bound.

annotations.label: Papadimitriou optimality of Cooley–Tukey additions

anchor.section: Bounds on complexity and operation counts
anchor.snippet: Papadimitriou (1979)

annotations.note: No formalization of Papadimitriou's optimality result for Cooley–Tukey additions.

annotations.label: Split-radix arithmetic complexity

anchor.section: Bounds on complexity and operation counts
anchor.snippet: the lowest published count for power-of-two

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize split-radix FFTs or their operation counts.

annotations.label: Sparse-input FFT complexity

anchor.snippet: if the data are sparse

annotations.note: Sparse FFT complexity (sFFT) is not present in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Cooley–Tukey relative error bound

anchor.snippet: The upper bound on the relative error for the Cooley–Tukey algorithm

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize floating-point error analysis of FFT algorithms.

annotations.label: Fixed-point Cooley–Tukey error growth

anchor.snippet: the finite-precision errors accumulated by FFT algorithms are worse

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize fixed-point arithmetic error bounds for FFTs.

annotations.label: Randomized FFT correctness verification

anchor.snippet: rigorous guarantees can be obtained in

annotations.note: No randomized FFT verification procedure is formalized in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Multidimensional DFT

anchor.snippet: the multidimensional DFT

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Analysis/Fourier/` defines DFT on `ZMod N` but not on products `ZMod N₁ × … × ZMod Nₖ`.

annotations.label: Row-column algorithm

anchor.snippet: the simplest and most common multidimensional DFT algorithm, known as the row-column algorithm

annotations.note: No row-column multidimensional DFT algorithm is formalized in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Vector-radix FFT algorithm

anchor.snippet: the vector-radix FFT algorithm , which is a generalization of the ordinary Cooley–Tukey algorithm

annotations.note: No vector-radix FFT in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Spherical harmonic FFT on S²

anchor.snippet: generalization to spherical harmonics on the sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib has no FFT formalization for spherical-harmonic expansions on the sphere.

annotations.label: Fast folding algorithm

anchor.snippet: The fast folding algorithm is analogous to the FFT

annotations.note: No fast folding algorithm is formalized in `Mathlib/`.

annotations.label: Quantum FFT

anchor.snippet: This is implemented as a sequence of 1- or 2-bit quantum gates now known as quantum FFT

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize quantum circuits or the quantum Fourier transform.
